mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises the relative attractiveness of fixed-income assets, causing capital reallocation away from equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of months when the federal funds rate is in its top quartile, or Rank IC remains above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income and consumption expectations, triggering downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and payroll employment indicate a broad-based demand collapse that is not captured by either series in isolation, generating strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and PAYEMS does not produce significantly lower 60-day returns than either series alone in a two-sample t-test at the 10% level, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, payroll employment growth slows as the labor market approaches structural capacity constraints; month-over-month deceleration in payrolls signals peaking corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when month-over-month PAYEMS growth decelerates by more than one standard deviation during late-cycle expansion windows, or Rank IC remains above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in housing starts reflects robust credit demand and construction sector confidence, which spills over into broader equity market sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns follow month-over-month increases in HOUST in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises in quarterly real GDP growth translate directly into upward earnings revisions and higher domestic demand absorption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns follow quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 accelerations in fewer than 53% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate a non-inflationary supply expansion that supports real earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration in consumer price inflation reduces the market-implied probability of near-term monetary tightening, lowering the equity risk premium and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns follow month-over-month CPIAUCSL decelerations in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
